revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ikune jinja (Sumiyoshi, Osaka)]][[ja:生根神社 (大阪市住吉区)]][[zh:生根神社 (大阪市住吉區)]] {{Expand Japanese|生根神社 (大阪市住吉区)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ikune Shrine | native_name = 生根神社 | image = Ikune-jinja (Sumiyoshi, Osaka), haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ikune Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.615438|135.493889}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|ca|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|fr|Sukunabikona|it|Sukuna-hikona|ja|スクナビコナ|ko|스쿠나비코나|mni|ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯕꯤꯀꯣꯅ|pl|Sukunabikona|pt|Sukunahikona|simple|Sukunabikona|sv|Suku-Na-Bikona|zh|少彥名|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} | website = {{Official website|http://ikune.a.la9.jp/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ikune Shrine'''|生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q134917288|ltq=Q134917288|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * offerings}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * Province|sk|Seccu (provincia)|sr|Провинција Сецу|tr|Settsu (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сеццу|zh_min_nan|Settu-kok|zh_yue|攝津國|zh|攝津國|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == different from (P1889) == * {{ill|Ikune Shrine|lt=Ikune Shrine|ja|生根神社 (大阪市西成区)|zh|生根神社 (大阪市西成区)|qid=Q11574576|1=Ikune Shrine (Nishinari Ward, Osaka City)}} == X (Twitter) username (P2002) == * ikunejinja ** P6552: 1365984972 ** Start time: {'time': '+2013-04-20T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** Point in time: {'time': '+2022-08-21T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8120005001809 == Commons category (P373) == * Ikune jinja (Sumiyoshi, Osaka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府大阪市住吉区住吉2丁目3-15', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Ikune jinja2.jpg {{commons category|Category:Ikune jinja (Sumiyoshi, Osaka)}} [[JA:生根神社 (大阪市住吉区)]] [[ZH:生根神社 (大阪市住吉區)]] {{wikidata link|Q11574575}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ikune Shrine |Image = [[File:Ikune-jinja (Sumiyoshi, Osaka), haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward|Q985702|qid=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}} Sumiyoshi 2-chome 3-15 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sukuna-bikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{Ublist| {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (major) | Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} }} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative names = Oku no Tenjin |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Ikune Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ikune Shrine''' (いくねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Sumiyoshi, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward|Q985702|qid=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In the past, it was a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}. It is commonly known as “Oku no Tenjin” or “Okutenjin.” === Enshrined deity === * Principal deity – {{ill|lt=Sukuna-bikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} Regarding the enshrined deity, works such as the '{{ill|lt=Settsu Meisho Zue|Q11498549|qid=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' and 'Sumiyoshi Meishō Zue' identify it as Sukunahikona no Mikoto, while the '{{ill|lt=Shinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|qid=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}' presents a theory that it is {{ill|lt=Ikutsuhikone|Q11147583|qid=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto|1=Ikutsuhikone}}{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Research Association|1977}}. === History === The precise date of foundation is unknownShrine history leaflet. According to shrine tradition, it predates the founding of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Research Association|1977}}, but the '{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|qid=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}' contains no mention of this shrine{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Research Association|1977}}. However, according to ancient tradition, since the shrine’s deity Sukunahikona no Mikoto is regarded as a god of sake brewing, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} is said to have brewed sake at this shrine and offered it to the {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Three Deities|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}{{Cite web |url=http://ikune.a.la9.jp/history |title=Shrine History |publisher=Ikune Shrine |access-date=2024-02-27}}. According to a document dated the first year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kyakufu-shō|Q11502312|qid=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}', one household from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} was assigned as a {{ill|lt=Shinbe (hereditary shrine households)|Q11589711|qid=Q11589711|1=Kanbe (social class)}} to “Ikune no Kami” at that time{{Sfn|Heibonsha Regional Data Center|1986}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in the fifth year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed as “Ikune Shrine, Major, Tsukinami and Niiname,” under {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi District|Q10885872|qid=Q10885872|1=Sumiyoshi district}} of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, placing it among the {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrines)|Q134917288|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}. It also records that the shrine received offerings at the imperial court’s {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{Sfn|Heibonsha Regional Data Center|1986}}. In the 14th year of {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} ({{ill|lt=1482|Q6633|qid=Q6633|1=1482}}), a Tenmangū shrine was established within the precincts. As this Tenmangū attracted popular devotion, Ikune Shrine itself came to be commonly known as “Oku no Tenjin.” The term “Oku” (“inner” or “deep”) derives from its location farther inland than {{ill|lt=Ōmi Shrine|Q11437478|qid=Q11437478|1=Daikai Shrine}} when viewed from Sumiyoshi Taisha{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Research Association|1977}}{{Sfn|Heibonsha Regional Data Center|1986}}. Alternatively, the name is also said to originate from “Oki no Amatsu-kami” (the offshore heavenly deity, Sukunahikona no Mikoto). From the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} ({{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|qid=Q6720|1=1600}}) to around the 6th year ({{ill|lt=1601|Q6725|qid=Q6725|1=1601}}), the main hall was rebuilt through a donation by {{ill|lt=Yodo-dono|Q247408|qid=Q247408|1=Yodo-dono}}, with {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|qid=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} serving as magistrate{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009350.html |publisher=Osaka City |title=Ikune Shrine (Oku no Tenjin), Tangible Cultural Property of Osaka Prefecture |access-date=2024-02-27}}{{Sfn|Heibonsha Regional Data Center|1986}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine functioned as a subsidiary shrine of Sumiyoshi Taisha{{Sfn|Heibonsha Regional Data Center|1986}}. The 'Sumiyoshi Matsuba Taiki' records it in the section on subsidiary shrines as “Okutenjin Shrine,” noting that shrine monks from the {{ill|lt=Jingū-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} of Sumiyoshi served there{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Research Association|1977}}. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}} (the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}), the shrine became independent from Sumiyoshi Taisha and was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|Heibonsha Regional Data Center|1986}}. Between {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) and {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|qid=Q2076|1=1911}} (Meiji 44), two Sae Shrines (both formerly unranked shrines), Ryūō Shrine (formerly unranked), and Tanekashi Shrine (formerly a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) were relocated into the precincts{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Research Association|1977}}. Foundation stones from the former Hobashira Kannon Hall, which had been built during the period of {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), are preserved within the precincts. === Shrine precincts === * Main Hall (Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible {{ill|lt=cultural property|Q858308|qid=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}}) – Rebuilt from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} ({{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|qid=Q6720|1=1600}}) to around the 6th year ({{ill|lt=1601|Q6725|qid=Q6725|1=1601}}) by donation of {{ill|lt=Yodo-dono|Q247408|qid=Q247408|1=Yodo-dono}}, with {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|qid=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} as magistrate. Built in the {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style, with a chidori karahafu gable on the front. The roof is {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} (cypress bark roofing). It preserves a complete {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Momoyama period|1=UNKNOWN}} architectural style{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Research Association|1977}}. * Worship Hall – Incorporates Momoyama-period architectural style; renovated in {{ill|lt=1936|Q18649|qid=Q18649|1=1936}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 11). * Kōbaiden – Said to be a relocated corridor of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Silla-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shinra-dera), the {{ill|lt=Jingū-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Research Association|1977}}. * Shrine office * West gate === Subsidiary shrines === '''Affiliated shrines''' * Tenmangū (Sugawara Shrine) – The wooden seated statue of Tenjin ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}), the sacred object, dates from the 14th year of {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} ({{ill|lt=1482|Q6633|qid=Q6633|1=1482}}) and is designated an Osaka City tangible cultural property. '''Minor shrines''' * Tenjō Inari Shrine * Ryūō Shrine * Sae Shrine – two sanctuaries * Tanekashi Shrine * Inari Shrine Ikune-jinja (Sumiyoshi, Osaka), honden.jpg|{{center|Main Hall}} Ikune-jinja (Sumiyoshi, Osaka), Tenmangu.jpg|{{center|Tenmangū}} Ikune-jinja (Sumiyoshi, Osaka), keidaisha.jpg|{{center|Precinct shrines}} Ikune-jinja (Sumiyoshi, Osaka), Inari-sha.jpg|{{center|Tenjō Inari Shrine}} === Festivals === The following festivals are held annually at Ikune Shrine{{Cite web |url=http://ikune.a.la9.jp/event/index.html |title=Festivals and Events |publisher=Ikune Shrine |access-date=2016-05-22}}. * Monthly Festival – 9th of every month * New Year’s Day Festival – January 1 * Setsubun Festival – February * Awashima Festival – April 1 * Tanabata Festival – July 7 * Summer Festival Eve – July 18 * Mikoshi Procession – July 19 * Annual Grand Festival – October 9 * Mikoshi Procession Festival – October * Children’s Festival – October * Shichi-Go-San pilgrimage – November * Daikon Cooking Festival – December * Year-end Festival – December 31 === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural properties === * Main Hall (structure) – Designated February 20, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|qid=Q2474|1=1970}} (Shōwa 45){{Efn2|name=一覧表}}. === Osaka City-designated tangible cultural properties === * Wooden seated statue of Tenjin (sculpture) – Sacred object of the Tenmangū in the precincts. Created in the 14th year of {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} ({{ill|lt=1482|Q6633|qid=Q6633|1=1482}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000117460.html |title=Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin |publisher=Osaka City |access-date=2016-05-22}}. Designated April 1, {{ill|lt=2011|Q1994|qid=Q1994|1=2011}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 23){{Efn2|name=一覧表|From the Osaka City file of the “List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture.”}}. === Osaka City-designated preserved woodland === * The {{ill|lt=Japanese holly|Q848316|qid=Q848316|1=Ilex integra}} trees of Ikune Shrine – The entire precinct is designated as sacred forest. === Address === * 2-3-15 Sumiyoshi, Sumiyoshi Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture === Access === * About 11 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Station|Q7637174|qid=Q7637174|1=Sumiyoshitaisha Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nankai Main Line|Q59453|qid=Q59453|1=Nankai Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nankai Electric Railway|Q1188230|qid=Q1188230|1=Nankai Electric Railway}} * About 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kohama Station|Q6425907|qid=Q6425907|1=Kohama Station}} on the Nankai Main Line * About 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-Higashi Station|Q7637175|qid=Q7637175|1=Sumiyoshihigashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nankai Kōya Line|Q71986|qid=Q71986|1=Koya Line}} * About 7 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kaminoki Stop|Q11588649|qid=Q11588649|1=Kaminoki Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankai Uemachi Line|Q5648519|qid=Q5648519|1=Hankai Uemachi Line}} * About 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Kohama Stop|Q5753654|qid=Q5753654|1=Higashi-Kohama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankai Line|Q5648522|qid=Q5648522|1=Hankai Line}} * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Stop (Osaka)|Q7637170|qid=Q7637170|1=Sumiyoshi Station (Osaka)}} on the Hankai Uemachi Line / Hankai Line === Notes === {{Footnote help}} === Annotations === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Further reading === * Shrine history leaflet “Brief History of Shikinaisha Ikune Shrine” * Explanatory signboard in the precincts * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Meiji Shrine History Materials Compilation Office|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine History Materials|Q11486103|qid=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine History Materials Compilation Office|year=1912|chapter=Ikune Shrine|ref=harv}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/227}} “Meiji Shrine History Materials: Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 1”] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 227. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Report on Shikinaisha Investigations|volume=Vol. 5 (Kyoto and Kinai 5)|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|date=1977-12|id={{JP number|78005760}}|chapter=Ikune Shrine|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heibonsha Regional Data Center|title={{ill|lt=Encyclopedia of Japanese Place Names|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}}|volume=28 (Osaka Prefecture Place Names 1)|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|date=1986-02|isbn=458249028X|chapter=Ikune Shrine|ref=harv}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ikune Shrine (Nishinari Ward, Osaka)|Q11574576|qid=Q11574576|lt=Ikune Shrine|1=Ikune Shrine (Nishinari Ward, Osaka City)}} – A divided spirit was enshrined from this shrine. * {{ill|lt=Yakuso-shin|Q11622504|qid=Q11622504|1=Three Medicine Gods}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ikune jinja (Sumiyoshi, Osaka)}} * {{Official website|url=https://www.ikunejinja.com/|name=Ikune Shrine}} – Official website * [Ikune Shrine] – Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines * [Ikune Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials” {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories ==
